bonuses.leadin: In a play written in part by an author with this surname, the “dupe” Portia tells a story about an infant and two monkeys that is so bad she is attacked by her audience. For 10 points each:

bonuses.leadin: This character causes confusion by using the phrase “done her in” when relating her suspicion that her aunt may have been murdered rather than dying of the flu. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this character who shocks her companions by saying that “If a man has a bit of a conscience, it always takes him when he’s sober” in explaining how some women are happy to have their husbands drink.
Eliza Doolittle meets Mrs. Higgins in the third act of this George Bernard Shaw play.

In the third act of Pygmalion, Henry Higgins takes some time before recalling that he met Freddy Eynsford-Hill in this place. Pygmalion begins in this district of London, which contains the Drury Lane theatre.
answers: Covent Garden [prompt on the West End of London]
answers_sanitized: Eliza Doolittle [or Eliza Doolittle; prompt on Doolittle]
Covent Garden [prompt on the West End of London]

bonuses.leadin: In early 20th century England, the theater in this location was the only opera venue to perform operas that weren't in English. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this district of London which is home to, and sometimes names, the Royal Opera House.

English opera houses experimented with these things in the 1920s, but they did not become widespread until the '80s. Foreign language films commonly use these things rather than dubbing over dialogue.

This opera house, notorious for its unruly and talkative audiences, became one of the first non-English opera houses to use surtitles when it adopted them in 1985. The opera Falstaff premiered here in 1893.

bonuses.leadin: This work is the basis for a ballet by Frederick Ashton that was premiered in 1968. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this work, formally titled Variations on an Original Theme, most of whose movements are labelled by initials such as G.R.S.
The ballet version of the Enigma Variations was premiered by the Royal Ballet at the Royal Opera House at this London location.
Covent Garden was notably the site of many of the premieres of this composer’s operas and oratorios, including a 1743 performance of Messiah.

bonuses.leadin: Philip Hensher wrote the libretto to this opera, which ends with an electrician and a maid destroying its setting, a lavish hotel room. 10 points each:
parts: Name this chamber opera by Thomas Adès, which musically depicts fellatio in its treatment of the “Dirty Duchess of Argyll.”
Another opera by Thomas Adès, The Tempest, premiered at this opera house in London. Its current music director is Sir Antonio Pappano.
In 1947, this George Bizet opera became the first full opera to be performed at the Covent Garden Opera House. Its musical numbers include the “Toreador Song.”
answers: Covent Garden Opera House [prompt on “Royal Opera House”]

bonuses.leadin: Anish Kapoor and Cecil Balmond recently designed the ArcelorMittal Orbit in this city. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this city that also contains Piccadilly Circus in its West End and Trafalgar Square.
Another square in London is this one laid out in piazza style by Inigo Jones. St. Paul’s Church, also designed by Jones, sits on end of this square, the first modern one in London.
While St. Paul’s Church in London was designed by Inigo Jones, St. Paul’s Cathedral on Ludgate Hill was designed by this man, who redesigned it after the Great Fire.
